content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

PILLSBURY, MADISON & SUTRO

OTHER OFFICES

POST OFFICE BOX 7880

LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA
SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA
WALNUT CREEK, CALIFORNIA
WASHINGTON, D.C.

SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94120

TELEPHONE (415) 983-1000

CABLE ADDRESS "EVANS"
TELEX 34743
TELECOPIER (415) 398-2096

WRITER'S OFFICE AND
DIRECT DIAL NUMBER

235 Montgomery Street
(415) 983-1585

January 30, 1991

Taxpayers to Limit Campaign
Spending v. FPPC - Status of
Propositions 68 and 73


VIA FEDERAL EXPRESS

John Wallace, Esq.
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Mr. Wallace:

     Further to our recent conversation, this letter is
a request for written advice pursuant to Government Code
section 83114(b).

     Please confirm my understanding of the following
based on our telephone conversation on January 23, 1991:

     1.  Effective with the denial of the petition for
reheating in Taxpayers to Limit Campaign
Spending v. FPPC, 51 Cal.3d 744, Supreme
Court No. S012106 (January 18, 1991)
("Taxpayers"), Taxpayers is now final.

     2.  Judge Karlton's partial stay in Service
Employees International Union, et al. v. Fair
Political Practices Commission, et al.,
No. CIV S-89-433 LKK (E.D.Cal., September 28,
1990) ("SEIU v. FPPC") dissolved as of the
date Taxpayers became final.

     3.  At present, there are no contribution limits
applicable in California except (1) the
limits contained in Government Code section

10601125

John Wallace, Esq.
January 30, 1991
Page 2

85305 with respect to special elections and
(2) any limits contained in valid local ordi-
nances.

4.    Transfers of funds by candidate controlled
committees to other candidate controlled
committees formed in connection with
(1) special elections or (2) elections held
in local jurisdictions with valid local or-
dinances remain banned under Government Code
section 85304 (notwithstanding SEIU v. FPPC,
747 F.Supp 580 (E.D.Cal. 1990)).

5.    No provision of Proposition 68 is, following
the decision in Taxpayers, presently being
enforced by the Commission.

We appreciate your attention to this matter. If
you have any questions, please contact me.

Very truly yours,

Frederick K. Lowell

10601125

embedding.qa_text: **January 30, 1991** Taxpayers to Limit Campaign Spending v. FPPC - Status of Propositions 68 and 73 VIA FEDERAL EXPRESS John Wallace, Esq. Staff Counsel, Legal Division Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, CA 95814 Dear Mr. Wallace: Further to our recent conversation, this letter is a request for written advice pursuant to Government Code section 83114(b). Please confirm my understanding of the following based on our telephone conversation on January 23, 1991: 1. Effective with the denial of the petition for rehearing in Taxpayers to Limit Campaign Spending v. FPPC, 51 Cal.3d 744, Supreme Court No. S012106 (January 18, 1991) ("Taxpayers"), Taxpayers is now final. 2. Judge Karlton's partial stay in Service Employees International Union, et al. v. Fair Political Practices Commission, et al., No. CIV S-89-433 LKK (E.D.Cal., September 28, 1990) ("SEIU v. FPPC") dissolved as of the date Taxpayers became final. 3. At present, there are no contribution limits applicable in California except (1) the limits contained in Government Code section 85305 with respect to special elections and (2) any limits contained in valid local ordinances. 4. Transfers of funds by candidate controlled committees to other candidate controlled committees formed in connection with (1) special elections or (2) elections held in local jurisdictions with valid local ordinances remain banned under Government Code section 85304 (notwithstanding SEIU v. FPPC, 747 F.Supp 580 (E.D.Cal. 1990)). 5. No provision of Proposition 68 is, following the decision in Taxpayers, presently being enforced by the Commission. We appreciate your attention to this matter. If you have any questions, please contact me. Very truly yours, Frederick K. Lowell
